multiplechoiceresponse.label: A parole dataset is defined to be α-biased if the difference in parole-granted rates between Group X and Group Y is at least α = 0.12. The dataset shows parole is granted to 35% of Group X and 20% of Group Y. Which statement best matches the lecture?

choicegroup.choice: The dataset is not α-biased because parole decisions are individualized.
The dataset becomes unbiased if group membership is excluded from the model.
The dataset is α-biased because the difference (0.15) is at least α = 0.12.
α-bias applies only after deployment due to feedback loops.

div.p: A dataset is defined to be α‑biased when the difference in positive outcome rates between two groups is at least α. α-bias is about the dataset’s outcome-rate gap across groups. The difference is 0.35 − 0.20 = 0.15. Since 0.15 ≥ 0.12, the dataset is α-biased by the provided definition. Feedback loops can amplify bias over time, but they are not required for α-bias to exist—α-bias can already be present in the historical data used for training.
